reasoning: Query produces incorrect results due to 3 critical semantic bugs (date encoding mismatch, output format, wrong join results). Must fix all critical_fixes first before performance work. With 2 remaining iterations and high-impact optimizations available (thread parallelism 50-60x, compact hash tables 2-4x), optimization is justified to close 8.78x performance gap.
focus_areas: Fix date encoding logic in filtering (year-only mismatch)
Fix date output formatting (convert integers to YYYY-MM-DD)
Verify and correct join/filter logic for correctness
Implement thread parallelism on 64-core CPU (50-60x potential speedup)
Replace std::unordered_map with compact hash tables (2-4x speedup)
strategy_notes: CORRECTNESS FIRST: All 3 critical_fixes must be applied and validated before further optimizations. After fixes, aggressive parallelism (morsel-driven, 64 threads) is the highest-impact optimization, followed by compact hash tables. I/O optimization and SIMD can be deferred to iter_2 if needed. Recommend: (1) Apply critical fixes, (2) Validate correctness, (3) Implement thread parallelism, (4) Revalidate, (5) Profile and apply remaining optimizations.
notes: Critical: Do not skip any of the 3 critical_fixes. Parallelism implementation should use std::thread::hardware_concurrency() for thread count and morsel-driven approach with atomic counter. After implementing fixes + parallelism, expected execution time should drop from 3250ms to ~100-150ms (after semantic fixes, parallelism brings to ~70ms baseline). This closes the gap to DuckDB's 99ms and meets GenDB baseline of 370ms.
